main.purpose: Primary response model — quality and identity fidelity matter most. Used for all user-facing replies across CLI, Web, and Telegram. Prices in BRL are approximate and used by the Context Rail to surface session cost; edit when provider rates shift.

reception.purpose: Classification of incoming messages across three independent axes — persona, organization, journey. Runs before every response to route the message. Haiku 4.5 was chosen over cheaper lite models because scope routing (CV1.E4.S1) exceeded the capacity of Gemini Flash Lite — the 3-axis prompt with ~17 candidates consistently missed sole-scope-in-domain cases. Haiku is ~10× pricier but still cheap in absolute terms (~R$0.002/message) and the routing quality pays for itself. Admins who need to cut cost and accept some missed scope routing can downgrade via /admin/models; reception falls back silently on any failure so a weaker model degrades gracefully. Prices in BRL are approximate.

title.purpose: Background summarization — generates a 3-6 word title for a session when the user ends it via 'Begin again'. Fire-and-forget: failure leaves the session untitled and the episodic browse falls back to 'Untitled conversation'. Speed and cost matter; semantic nuance is less critical than a decent-enough label.
